text.title: Reporting Structure and Review Process
text.summary: Monthly metrics tracker structure, report format, and review cadence rules.

rule.statement: Review dashboards on a fixed cadence: daily pulse check (2 min), weekly review (30 min), monthly report (2 hours). Skip reviews create metric blindspots.
rule.rationale: The purpose of a dashboard is to catch problems early. A dashboard reviewed monthly catches problems a month after they start. A dashboard reviewed daily catches them on day 2.

list.item: Daily: pulse check — is anything broken? (revenue, signups, errors)
Weekly: trend review — are key metrics moving in the right direction?
Monthly: full analysis — channel performance, cohort comparison, targets vs actuals
Quarterly: target reset — update benchmarks based on current trajectory

rule.statement: Set automated alerts for metric deviations greater than 20% from the 7-day rolling average. Alert on drops, not just on targets.
rule.rationale: Target-based alerts ("below $X MRR") miss sudden drops that remain above target. Deviation-based alerts catch anomalies regardless of absolute level.

section.title: Template References
section.reference: Spreadsheet structure for revenue, customers, unit economics, marketing
Dashboard specification: purpose, audience, metrics, visualizations, filters
Monthly report: executive summary, KPIs, channel performance, insights
